title: CBOE Equity Put/Call Ratio (PCR) Daily Market Sentiment Gate
summary: Download the CBOE daily equity put/call ratio from CBOE's free historical data page (cboe.com/us/options/market_statistics/historical_data) and use it as a daily market sentiment gate. The equity PCR baseline is approximately 0.7 (calls dominate). PCR >1.1 indicates elevated put buying relative to calls — historically a contrarian bullish signal as fear peaks. PCR <0.5 signals extreme complacency (too many calls vs puts) and is a caution flag for new long entries. QuantifiedStrategies backtested a simple PCR strategy (buy when equity PCR >1.1, hold N days) and found statistically significant positive forward returns over a 20+ year window — particularly robust as a confirmation filter rather than a standalone signal.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — Backtesting Robustness, Walk-Forward Validation & Intraday Timing Signals
expected_impact: +2–3% win rate by filtering out entries during extreme market complacency (low PCR) when downside risk is highest; contrarian PCR >1.1 is a documented precursor to market rallies; equity baseline ~0.7 (source: CBOE 2025)
deferral_reason: Covered by research-options-flow-proxy — deferred during paper_trading_single_account prioritisation
